headerTitle: Prompt Systems Engineer  ·  LLM Production Systems  ·  Evaluation Frameworks  ·  Claude Daily  ·  Agentic AI
subjectText: Prompt Systems Engineer — Noodle (Remote) · $115K–$130K — Will Austin
intro: Noodle is looking for someone who has designed and shipped production prompt systems — not experimented with them, but built the evaluation layer, caught the edge cases before deployment, and iterated against real users in real workflows. That is the day-to-day across 14 live AI endpoints: system prompt architecture, multi-turn conversation logic, structured output contracts, and the guardrail patterns that keep agents behaving correctly when the conversation goes sideways.
bullets: Production prompt engineering at scale — 14 live AI endpoints built and maintained in Claude Code daily: system prompt design, persona definition, multi-turn conversation logic, chain-of-thought patterns, and RAG integration across enrollment, support, and operational workflows. Every prompt ships with an output contract — Zod-validated JSON schema that fails loudly on hallucination or format drift before it reaches a user.

Evaluation frameworks and quality systems — every production AI system ships with a pre-deployment test suite: adversarial inputs designed to surface edge cases, structured output validation, HITL guardrails for low-confidence responses, and per-request observability logging. The evaluation layer is not an afterthought — it is built before the prompt goes to production.

Red-teaming and adversarial testing — designed and ran adversarial test scenarios across multi-turn agent workflows: out-of-scope queries, persona drift, conflicting instructions, injection attempts. Built the failure taxonomy before the agent met real users. The goal is finding the breaking point in staging, not in production.

Cross-functional delivery with non-technical stakeholders — four complete external client engagements translating operational requirements into agent behavior: roofing, home services, auto repair, OOH advertising. Each started with a workflow audit and ended with a production AI system the client operated without technical support.
closingLine: The prompt engineering discipline Noodle is hiring for — production system design, evaluation frameworks, adversarial testing, and cross-functional delivery against real operational requirements — is the current daily operating model. I would welcome a conversation about where the AI orchestration platform is headed and what the highest-leverage prompt system opportunities look like in the enrollment and student support workflows.
matchNotes: Strong: production prompt engineering (14 endpoints), evaluation frameworks (Zod validation + HITL), red-teaming patterns, multi-turn conversation logic, cross-functional stakeholder delivery. Gaps: education/LMS domain (Canvas/Moodle), Langfuse specifically, $115-130K below prior floor but floor dropped. Apply on prompt engineering depth.
